LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WALGREENS CO.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED, WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO STATE THE DATES WHEN PLAINTIFF WAS ALLEGEDLY INJURED OR FIRST DISCOVERED SHE WAS INJURED. MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-18-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT WALGREENS CO., INCORRECTLY SUED AS WALGREENS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. A COURT MAY TAKE JUDICIAL NOTICE OF INCONSISTENT STATEMENTS BY PLAINTIFF IN PLEADINGS FOR PURPOSES OF A DEMURRER. (OWENS V. KING SUPERMARKET (1988) 198 CAL.APP.3D 379, 384-85.) IN THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT, PLAINTIFF PLED THAT SHE NOTICED DEFENDANT'S MISTAKE ON JANUARY 16, 2012. (COMPLAINT, CAUSE OF ACTION 1 ATTACHMENT.) IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, PLAINTIFF PLED THAT SHE DID NOT KNOW ABOUT DEFENDANT'S MISTAKE UNTIL MARCH 22, 2012. (SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, 12.) A PLAINTIFF CAN EXPLAIN IN A SUBSEQUENT COMPLAINT WHY THE EARLIER STATEMENTS ARE INCORRECT. IF A PLAINTIFF FAILS TO DO SO, HOWEVER, THE STATEMENTS IN THE EARLIER PLEADING ARE "READ INTO" THE COMPLAINT, AND ALLEGATIONS INCONSISTENT THEREWITH ARE TREATED AS SHAM AND DISREGARDED. (OWENS, 198 CAL.APP.3D AT 384.) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SUFFICIENTLY EXPLAINED WHY THE STATEMENT IN THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT IS INCORRECT. THEREFORE, THE JANUARY 16, 2012 DATE STATED IN THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT IS "READ INTO" THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. AS SUCH, THE ONE-YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS FOR PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE SET FORTH IN CCP 340.5 HAS RUN AND PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS BARRED AS UNTIMELY. PLAINTIFF IS GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO FURTHER EXPLAIN WHY THE STATEMENT IN THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT IS INCORRECT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT WALGREENS CO., INCORRECTLY SUED AS WALGREENS' DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT - JUDGE BUSCH IS RECUSED. THIS MATTER WILL BE HEARD BY JUDGE GOLDSMITH ON MARCH 4, 2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WALGREENS CO., INCORRECTLY SUED AS WALGREENS' DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT ("TAC") SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS FACTS EXPLAINING WHY THE STATEMENT IN THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT IS INCORRECT. (TAC, 11-16.) THE TAC PLEADS THAT PLAINTIFF COULD NOT HAVE REASONABLY DISCOVERED THE WRONGFUL CAUSE OF HER INJURIES UNTIL MARCH 22, 2012. (TAC, 11-16.) THE FILING OF THE COMPLAINT ON FEBRUARY 26, 2013 WAS THEREFORE WITHIN THE ONE-YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS. (CCP SEC. 340.5.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT WALGREENS CO., INCORRECTLY SUED AS WALGREENS' DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is Overruled. The Third Amended Complaint ("TAC") sufficiently pleads facts explaining why the statement in the original Complaint is incorrect. (TAC, 11-16.) The TAC pleads that Plaintiff could not have reasonably discovered the wrongful cause of her injuries until March 22, 2012. (TAC, 11-16.) The filing of the complaint on February 26, 2013 was therefore within the one-year statute of limitations. (CCP sec. 340.5.) (Prevailing party to submit a proposed order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-30-2013 Continued To Apr-16-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-13-2013 Continued To Feb-28-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-30-2013 Continued To Jan-15-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-18-2013 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. rULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WALGREENS CO., INCORRECTLY SUED AS WALGREENS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. A court may take judicial notice of inconsistent statements by plaintiff in pleadings for purposes of a demurrer. (Owens v. King Supermarket (1988) 198 Cal.App.3d 379, 384-85.) In the original complaint, Plaintiff pled that she noticed Defendant's mistake on January 16, 2012. (Complaint, Cause of Action 1 Attachment.) In the Second Amended Complaint, Plaintiff pled that she did not know about Defendant's mistake until March 22, 2012. (Second Amended Complaint, 12.) A plaintiff can explain in a subsequent complaint why the earlier statements are incorrect. If a plaintiff fails to do so, however, the statements in the earlier pleading are "read into" the complaint, and allegations inconsistent therewith are treated as sham and disregarded. (Owens, 198 Cal.App.3d at 384.) Plaintiff has not sufficiently explained why the statement in the original complaint is incorrect. Therefore, the January 16, 2012 date stated in the original complaint is "read into" the Second Amended Complaint. As such, the one-year statute of limitations for professional negligence set forth in CCP 340.5 has run and Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint is barred as untimely. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to further explain why the statement in the original complaint is incorrect. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-28-2013 Continued To Nov-13-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANT WALGREENS CO.'s DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted, with 10 days leave to amend to state the dates when Plaintiff was allegedly injured or first discovered she was injured. (See CCP 430.10(f).) The First Amended Complaint is uncertain, particularly in light of the allegations in the original Complaint. No opposition filed. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
